name: llm-memory.net
description: Persistent memory platform with semantic search, multi-agent communication (mail, chat, structured discussions with voting), admin dashboard, and MCP integration.
approachDetail: Notes stored in PostgreSQL, chunked by markdown headings, embedded via OpenAI text-embedding-3-small, searched with pgvector cosine similarity. Filename boosting ranks matches from relevant source files higher.
searchDetail: Semantic search via pgvector cosine similarity with filename boosting. Keyword search via case-insensitive grep across notes with context lines.
storageDetail: PostgreSQL 17 with pgvector extension. Notes, vectors, agent state, mail, chat, discussions, and config all in one database.
mcpDetail: Native MCP server with Streamable HTTP transport. HMAC OAuth authentication.
toolSupportDetail: Hosted service at llm-memory.net or self-hosted.
scopesDetail: Namespace-based isolation. Each agent owns a namespace with full access. Cross-namespace read/write via explicit permissions. Shared namespaces for cross-agent knowledge.
duplicateDetail: Save-by-slug upsert model — notes are created or updated by slug. Vector chunks are replaced on each save. No automatic dedup across different slugs.
retentionDetail: Soft delete with restore. Notes persist until explicitly deleted. Cron purges expired sessions and old request logs.
multiUserDetail: Multi-agent architecture. Each agent authenticates independently with its own namespace. Agents communicate via mail, chat, and structured discussions with voting.
crossMachineDetail: Server-hosted — agents on different machines connect to the same API.
webUiDetail: Vue 3 admin dashboard for notes, agents, activity, virtual agents, mail/chat history, and namespace permissions.
tokenBudgetingDetail: Cost-based budgeting for virtual agents — daily and monthly dollar limits per agent. Fail-closed on unknown pricing.
contextInjectionDetail: Agents retrieve memories via MCP tool calls (search, read_note, grep). No automatic injection.
networkDetail: Requires network connection to llm-memory.net or self-hosted instance. No offline mode.
auditTrailDetail: API request logging, mail history with delivery status, soft delete/restore on notes. No full event sourcing.
pros: Hosted service with zero setup, or self-hostable via Ansible
Multi-agent communication: async mail, real-time chat, structured discussions with voting
Admin dashboard for browsing, editing, and searching all stored knowledge
Namespace-based access control with cross-namespace permissions
Virtual agent system with cost-based budgeting
cons: Requires PostgreSQL with pgvector for self-hosting
No automatic deduplication or contradiction detection across notes
Requires OpenAI API for embeddings (no local embedding option)
notes: Also functions as a multi-agent coordination platform. Agents can hold formal discussions, propose and vote on decisions, and communicate asynchronously.
